subject: Polyglot architecture decisions (Rust core + Python wrapper)
summary: Confirmed phenoVessel/phenoTypes already deleted by upstream; sharecli and thegent-sharecli are complementary (different abstraction layers, not duplicates).

decisions.rationale: Already 404. Successor: PhenoPlugins/crates/pheno-plugin-vessel/ (6,434 LoC: 3,687 prod + 2,747 tests).
Already 404. Successor: HexaKit/python/ (9,543 LoC across 8 sub-packages).
Process-level manager. OS process pool via sysinfo, project limits, resource checks. 1,583 LoC production + 1 inline test.
Coordination-level manager. In-memory locks, Maildir-style queue, Mergiraf, HLC. 1,014 LoC + 109 tests (10.7% ratio, below org standards).

pattern.name: PRCP
pattern.explanation: Rust crate handles the OS-level process management (fast, low-level); Python crate handles the higher-level coordination (locks, queues, merge semantics).

polyglot_pairs_observed.alignment: field-name parity gap (Rust has url/port/log_level/db_path/feature_flags; Python has environment/debug/service_name)
Python core + Rust pprof-rs bridge (proposed in Profila migration)
Rust for OTel SDK bindings, Python for performance analysis

adrs_proposed: ADR-D1: phenoVessel deprecation complete (write-only, low priority)
ADR-D2: phenoTypes deprecation complete (write-only, low priority)
future_hardening: thegent-sharecli: rename LockStatus/QueuePriority collision (defined twice each)
